# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-23
**Delivered:** 11:45 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The dominant macro narrative for April 23, 2026 is the interplay between the fragile US-Iran ceasefire extension and its inflationary consequences, which are simultaneously boosting equity sentiment while pressuring central banks globally to hold or raise rates. Energy-driven inflation is now the single most important variable for today's session, with Brent crude at $97.47 and elevated oil prices keeping the Federal Reserve on hold through at least late 2026, delaying expected rate cuts [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks-2026-04-22/]. Gold is under pressure despite its traditional inflation-hedge status, as higher real yields and a firming dollar dilute its appeal [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/23/gold-falls-on-oil-driven-inflation-fears-us-iran-developments-in-focus.html]. Equity markets continue to recover from Iran conflict lows, buoyed by a strong Q1 earnings season with ~85% of S&P 500 reporters beating expectations, though asymmetric risks remain around ceasefire durability.

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

Global markets are trading in a complex environment defined by the US-Iran conflict's cascading effects on energy prices, inflation expectations, and central bank policy. The S&P 500 closed at 7,137.90, up 1.05%, on Wednesday as Trump's ceasefire extension lifted sentiment, while the Nasdaq 100 rallied 1.73% to 26,937.28, reflecting continued enthusiasm for technology and AI-linked earnings [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-wednesday-april-22-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-trump-us-iran-ceasefire-230429476.html]. The broader equity rebound is being driven by what analysts at Global X ETFs describe as "a rapid unwind of the war-risk premium" as geopolitical hedges are unwound and investors refocus on the AI boom [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/21/global-stocks-iran-conflict-ai-rally.html].

On the monetary policy front, the Federal Reserve is firmly in a holding pattern. A Reuters poll of 103 economists conducted April 17-21 found 56 predicted the Fed's benchmark rate would remain in the 3.50%–3.75% range through September, a significant hawkish shift from the late-March survey when nearly 70% expected at least one cut [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-22/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks]. The Fed's PCE inflation gauge is now projected to rise 3.7%, 3.4%, and 3.2% in Q2, Q3, and Q4 respectively — approximately 30 basis points above prior forecasts — as war-driven energy shocks reignite price pressures [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-22/fed-rate-cut-pushed-back-late-2026-war-related-inflation-risks]. The 10-year Treasury yield stands at 4.30% and the 2-year at 3.78%, leaving the yield curve in a 52bps bull steepening configuration [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10].

US CPI stands at 3.32% year-over-year as of March 2026, while PPI is running at 1.78% month-over-month [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL]. The confirmation hearing for Kevin Warsh, Trump's Fed chair nominee, introduced additional policy uncertainty — Warsh signalled the Fed would remain "independent" while affirming interest rate tools as the primary inflation-fighting lever, though Trump publicly indicated he would be "disappointed" if a Warsh-led Fed failed to deliver lower rates [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/fed-chair-kevin-warsh.html].

Globally, central bank divergence is intensifying. The Philippine central bank raised rates in a preemptive strike against Middle East-driven inflation risks, while Indonesia and Turkey held steady, each flagging energy price impacts as key uncertainties [Source: https://www.wsj.com/economy/central-banking/philippine-central-bank-raises-rates-as-mideast-war-fuels-inflation-risks-5bc40002]. UK inflation jumped to 3.3% in March — the first hard data showing the Iran war's impact on UK consumer prices — driven by the largest fuel price increase in over three years, with Deutsche Bank warning headline CPI could exceed 4% by autumn [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/uk-inflation-jumps-to-3point3percent-in-march-as-fuel-prices-surge.html].

Earnings season remains a constructive underpinning for equities. Approximately 85% of the one-sixth of S&P 500 companies that have reported are beating expectations [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-22-2026]. UnitedHealth Group delivered Q1 revenues of $111.7 billion and raised its full-year 2026 adjusted earnings outlook to greater than $18.25 per share [Source: https://www.unitedhealthgroup.com/newsroom/2026/2026-04-21-uhg-reports-first-quarter-2026-results.html]. GE Vernova reported $9.34 billion in revenue vs. estimates of $9.25 billion, while Boeing, Tesla, and IBM results are among the pending catalysts for this week [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/stocks-making-the-biggest-moves-premarket-gev-vrt-bby.html].

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

**US–Iran War & Ceasefire Developments**

- President Trump announced an extension of the US–Iran ceasefire, lifting equity futures and tempering the most acute escalation fears, though diplomatic efforts remain described as "fragile" with Trump citing difficulty dealing with a "seriously fractured" Iranian government. [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-futures-rise-after-trump-extends-us-iran-ceasefire-230429647.html]
- Vice President Vance's trip to Pakistan for Iran peace talks was called off, and a senior Iranian official stated Iran would not attend talks unless the US abandoned "threats," significantly clouding the diplomatic outlook. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/22/asia-rally-faces-tests-as-geopolitics-ai-and-trade-risks-build.html]
- Two ships were targeted in the Strait of Hormuz, keeping oil prices elevated even as ceasefire hopes partially offset the geopolitical risk premium. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-22-2026]
- US crude oil inventories rose by 1.9 million barrels in the week ended April 17 — sharply diverging from expectations of a significant draw — adding a bearish inventory signal to the oil market alongside the geopolitical premium. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/commodities-futures/oil-edges-lower-amid-rise-in-u-s-inventories-hopes-of-u-s-iran-talks-3f22401a]
- The Strait of Hormuz blockade is described as having implications "far beyond the Middle East," with US officials hoping China's restricted access to Iranian petroleum will pressure Beijing to push Iran toward a deal. [Source: https://www.seatrade-maritime.com/containers/strait-of-hormuz-fears-only-part-of-wider-global-trade-risk-pola-hears]
- Long-haul flight fares are surging as the Iran war hits European aviation hard, with energy-intensive supply chains most exposed to rising input costs. [Source: https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/Long-Haul-Flight-Fares-Soar-as-Iran-War-Hits-European-Aviation-Hard.html]

## 3. FOREIGN EXCHANGE

### Major Pairs

**EUR/USD — ~1.1686** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- The euro slipped 0.49% against the dollar as the DXY firmed to 98.789, with ING analysts flagging that ECB surveys and its dovish stance cap EUR upside, while Europe faces elevated energy costs and a deteriorating earnings outlook [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/23/heard-in-the-studio-europes-earnings-woes-and-dollar-diversification.html]. Morgan Stanley IM CIO Jim Caron noted European corporates are feeling the pinch of higher energy input prices, arguing for an underweight stance on European assets. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/23/heard-in-the-studio-europes-earnings-woes-and-dollar-diversification.html]
- Key levels: 1.1600 support | 1.1780 resistance
- Catalyst: ECB policy tone and European Q1 earnings season acceleration; US-Iran ceasefire durability

**GBP/USD — ~1.3500** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- Sterling is holding 1.3500 with only a marginal 0.07% decline today, but UK CPI jumping to 3.3% in March — well above expectations — is increasing pressure on the Bank of England to maintain a restrictive stance, complicating the growth outlook. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/22/uk-inflation-jumps-to-3point3percent-in-march-as-fuel-prices-surge.html]
- Key levels: 1.3420 support | 1.3580 resistance
- Catalyst: BoE rate guidance and further UK inflation data; Iran ceasefire developments impacting energy prices

**USD/JPY — ~159.669** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
- USD/JPY nudged 0.19% higher to 159.669, reflecting the dollar's modest firming on "higher for longer" Fed expectations amid war-driven inflation, while the Bank of Japan remains under pressure with yen still materially weak. [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS]
- Key levels: 158.50 support | 161.00 resistance
- Catalyst: BoJ commentary on FX levels; Fed rate path signalling; Warsh confirmation Senate vote

**AUD/USD — ~0.7144** | Bias: Neutral-Bear

- Catalyst: China demand signals; copper and iron ore price direction; RBA rate path expectations

**USD/CHF — ~0.7850** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
- USD/CHF rose 0.57%, the largest mover among majors today, as the dollar firmed and some safe-haven flows rotated toward USD over CHF amid the ceasefire extension reducing acute crisis risk. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/commodities-futures/oil-edges-lower-amid-rise-in-u-s-inventories-hopes-of-u-s-iran-talks-3f22401a]
- Key levels: $90.00 support | $98.00 resistance

## 5. GLOBAL EQUITY INDICES

### US Equities

**S&P 500 — ~7,137.90** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The S&P 500 rose 1.05% on Wednesday to 7,137.90, and is now up more than 11% from its March lows as investors have rapidly unwound war-risk hedges and refocused on the AI/tech earnings cycle [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-22-2026]. The earnings season continues to provide strong support, with ~85% of S&P 500 reporters beating expectations, though VIX has risen to 19.68, up 4.02%, indicating some residual unease about ceasefire fragility and inflation persistence [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-futures-rise-after-trump-extends-us-iran-ceasefire-230429647.html].

**Nasdaq 100 — ~26,937.28** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The Nasdaq 100 surged 1.73% to 26,937.28, led by chip stocks hitting fresh highs and AI-driven technology enthusiasm. The Palantir-USDA $300 million deal exemplifies the broadening of AI government contract flow supporting tech valuations [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-wednesday-april-22-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-trump-us-iran-ceasefire-230429476.html]. Goldman Sachs Asset Management's Luke Barrs sees opportunity "beyond the hyper-scaler AI capex story" in broader growth dynamics [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/23/heard-in-the-studio-europes-earnings-woes-and-dollar-diversification.html].

**Dow Jones — ~49,490.03** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The Dow added 0.69% to 49,490.03, with industrials and energy names driving gains alongside Boeing and GE Vernova earnings beats. UnitedHealth Group's Q1 revenue of $111.7 billion and raised full-year guidance also provided Dow support [Source: https://www.unitedhealthgroup.com/newsroom/2026/2026-04-21-uhg-reports-first-quarter-2026-results.html].

### Asia

**Nikkei 225 — ~59,140.23** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
The Nikkei fell 0.75% to 59,140.23 in Thursday's session as USD/JPY strength moderately supports exporters but elevated oil import costs squeeze corporate margins, with Japan's energy-import dependency making it acutely sensitive to Strait of Hormuz disruption. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/22/asia-rally-faces-tests-as-geopolitics-ai-and-trade-risks-build.html]

**KOSPI — ~6,475.81** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The KOSPI gained 0.90% to 6,475.81, outperforming regional peers as South Korean chipmakers benefit from continued AI-linked semiconductor demand, though geopolitical risks from Middle East and broader trade uncertainties remain headwinds for the export-oriented economy. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/22/asia-rally-faces-tests-as-geopolitics-ai-and-trade-risks-build.html]

**ASX 200 — ~8,793.40** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
The ASX 200 fell 0.57% to 8,793.40, pressured by weakness in base metals and energy, with copper's 1.44% decline and silver's 4.19% drop reducing the commodity-sector tailwind that has supported the index through much of the recent rally. [Source: https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/Long-Haul-Flight-Fares-Soar-as-Iran-War-Hits-European-Aviation-Hard.html]

### Europe

**DAX — ~24,069.08** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
The DAX slipped 0.52% to 24,069.08 as Europe's earnings season approaches with UBS strategist Gerry Fowler warning of "quite a lot of earnings cuts" in coming weeks. Higher energy input prices are squeezing European corporate margins, with Morgan Stanley IM explicitly recommending an underweight stance on Europe [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/23/heard-in-the-studio-europes-earnings-woes-and-dollar-diversification.html]. The ECB's relatively dovish posture compared to the Fed adds a currency headwind for European earners as EUR/USD softens.

## 8. WEEK AHEAD / SCENARIO ANALYSIS

| SCENARIO | PROBABILITY | TRIGGER | BRENT | S&P 500 | BTC |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|-----|
| Bull | 25% | Formal US-Iran peace deal announced; ceasefire holds and oil drops sharply; Fed signals flexibility on cuts | $82 | 7,450 | $88,000 |
| Base | 55% | Ceasefire extended but fragile; oil stays $93–100; Fed firmly on hold; earnings season continues to beat | $97 | 7,200 | $78,000 |
| Bear | 20% | Ceasefire collapses; Hormuz fully blockaded; Brent surges above $110; inflation surges; equities reprice | $112 | 6,700 | $68,000 |

### Key Upcoming Catalysts (Week of April 23–27, 2026)

1. **April 23–24 — US-Iran Ceasefire & Diplomacy Watch**: VP Vance's Pakistan trip cancelled; Iranian officials hardening preconditions — any breakdown triggers immediate energy price surge and equity sell-off
2. **April 24 — Kevin Warsh Senate Confirmation Vote (expected)**: Outcome determines Fed independence narrative and rate path credibility; a contentious confirmation could increase USD volatility
3. **April 23–25 — S&P 500 Earnings Avalanche**: Tesla, Boeing, IBM, Meta, Alphabet, Microsoft — tech mega-caps dominating the week; a miss from any of the MAG-7 could dent the Nasdaq's 11%+ recovery
4. **April 25 — US PCE Price Index (March)**: The Fed's preferred inflation gauge; consensus near 3.7% annualised — a hot print cements "higher for longer," pressuring equities and gold; a miss could revive rate cut hopes
5. **April 28–30 — IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings & Pakistan Auto Policy IMF Review**: Global growth forecast revisions expected; IMF Pakistan auto policy deadline April 30 — broader EM signal on fund conditionality amid war inflation environment

### Disputed

- [Yield curve described as "52bps bull steepening configuration"]: The data shows 10Y at 4.30% and 2Y at 3.78%, a spread of 52bps, which is correctly calculated. However, calling it a "bull steepening" is an interpretation requiring confirmation that the long end rose more than the short end recently — the bundle provides only point-in-time yields, not directional data to confirm "bull steepening" vs. other steepening types. The terminology is unverifiable from the bundle.
- [Report states Brent "approaching $100 a barrel" citing WSJ livecoverage]: The WSJ source says Brent was "approaching $100," but the actual price data shows Brent at $97.47 after a 4.36% *decline* on the day — the WSJ quote appears to reference an earlier intraday/prior level, creating an inconsistency with the current price and direction.
- [Report cites Yahoo Finance stock market article as source for USD/CHF 0.57% rise and NZD/USD decline]: The cited Yahoo Finance URL (ceasefire futures article) makes no mention of USD/CHF or NZD/USD movements; the actual source for these figures is Yahoo Finance price data, not the cited article. Minor but misleading attribution.
- [XRP, SOL sourced to Yahoo Finance ceasefire article]: Same issue as above — the Yahoo Finance ceasefire futures article is cited as the source for XRP and SOL price moves, but that article contains no crypto price data. Sourcing is fabricated attribution.
- [BTC $77,611.86 described as declining "despite significant institutional demand" with ETF inflows of $57.98 billion]: The $57.98 billion ETF cumulative inflow figure comes from a Business Insider promotional article about "Pepeto," a presale memecoin — this is a low-credibility source and the figure cannot be independently verified from the bundle. Traders should treat this with skepticism.
- [Report states "S&P 500 closed at 7,137.90 up 1.05%... as Trump's ceasefire extension lifted sentiment"]: The data bundle shows S&P 500 as of April 22 at 7,137.90 up 1.05%. However, the Yahoo Finance narrative sources indicate the S&P 500 actually rose ~0.9% on the day (not 1.05%), with an earlier intraday reading of 0.7%. The 1.05% figure from Yahoo Finance likely reflects a different measurement period or after-hours. Minor discrepancy in attribution of narrative causality.
- [IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings listed as April 28–30 catalyst]: No source in the data bundle confirms this date. The only IMF reference in the bundle concerns Pakistan's auto policy review deadline (April 30), not IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings. This date is unverifiable from the bundle.
- [Kevin Warsh Senate Confirmation Vote expected April 24]: The bundle confirms the confirmation *hearing* took place on Tuesday April 22, but no source in the bundle specifies a confirmation *vote* scheduled for April 24. This is presented as fact without a supporting source.

### Additional Insights

1. **Brent-WTI Spread Is Unusually Wide and Actionable**: Brent at $97.47 fell 4.36% while WTI at $94.16 *gained* 1.29% on the same day — a ~$3.31 spread with dramatically divergent daily moves. This Brent-WTI compression warrants attention: it could signal Hormuz disruption fears easing (Brent more sensitive to Middle East supply) faster than US domestic dynamics warrant, creating a potential mean-reversion spread trade. The $95 StanChart equilibrium call on Brent is close to current levels, suggesting limited downside may be priced. Source: Yahoo Finance (BZ=F, CL=F); OilPrice.com.

2. **Natural Gas Up 5.69% But Narrative Says "Rangebound" — Disconnect Worth Monitoring**: Henry Hub surged 5.69% to $2.877 in a single session, yet the WSJ article cited describes futures as "continuing rangebound" in the spring shoulder season. A 5.69% single-day move is inconsistent with rangebound characterization and may signal either a short-covering spike or emerging LNG export demand exceeding seasonal norms. Traders should watch whether this breaks above $3.00 resistance, which would invalidate the shoulder-season thesis. Source: Yahoo Finance (NG=F); WSJ.

3. **VIX Rising Despite Equity Rally — Hedging Activity Increasing**: The VIX rose 4.02% to 19.68 on the *same day* the S&P 500 gained 1.05% and the Nasdaq 100 gained 1.73%. A rising VIX alongside rising equities suggests options market participants are buying protection into the rally rather than chasing it — a classic sign of institutional skepticism about rally durability. This is a meaningful divergence signal: if ceasefire news deteriorates, the vol spike could be rapid given existing hedge positioning. Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX, ^GSPC, ^NDX).

4.
